sidebar.clearCredentialsDescription: This deletes the locally saved Client ID/Secret and access token. You will be signed out now and will need to redo the Google credentials setup to use the app again. Classes and materials already synced remain in the local database.

courses.title: Classes
courses.syncError: Couldn't sync your classes. Try again.
courses.noActiveClassesHint: Sync your Google Classroom classes to get started.

deadlines.title: Deadlines
deadlines.syncError: Couldn't sync your deadlines. Try again.

downloads.title: Downloads
downloads.clearDescription: Removes every item from the list. Files already downloaded stay in Materials/ — this only clears the history, nothing is deleted from disk.

signIn.tagline: Organize classes, materials, and deadlines from Google Classroom in one place.

setup.title: Connect your Google account
setup.intro: Augmented Google Classroom has no central server — each install uses its own Google Cloud credentials. This is done once, takes about 5 minutes, and is saved locally.
setup.step1Body: Go to the <link>Google Cloud Console</link> and create a new project (you can name it "Augmented Google Classroom"), or select an existing project from the picker at the top of the page.
setup.step2Body: In the API Library, search for and click Enable on:
setup.step2Drive: Google Drive API (needed to download material attachments)
setup.step3Title: Step 3 — Configure the Google Auth Platform
setup.step3Body: Google reorganized the old single-page "OAuth consent screen" into tabs, under <link>Google Auth Platform</link>. Fill these in this order:
setup.step3BrandingItem: Branding tab: app name, support email, and developer contact email.
setup.step3UserType: Audience tab — User type: External (unless you have Google Workspace).
setup.step3TestUsers: Still on the Audience tab, under Test users, add your own Google email. Without this, Google blocks sign-in with the error "access blocked: app has not completed verification" — you do not need to publish the app or complete formal verification, just register the emails that will use it here.
setup.step3ScopesIntro: Data Access tab → Add or remove scopes → manually add all of these (without this, the scope won't even show up as grantable on the consent screen):
setup.step4Body: On the Clients tab, click Create Credentials → OAuth client ID:
setup.step4ClientCreate: Click Create — a modal will show the Client ID and the Client Secret.
setup.errorMissingFields: Fill in both fields to continue.

settings.title: Settings
settings.languageIntro: Choose the language used throughout the app.
settings.lyceumIntro: Connect your Lyceum account to bring grades and academic history into the app. Login opens a Chrome window so you can authenticate directly on your institution's portal.
settings.tenantHint: This is the prefix of your Lyceum portal address (https://'<institution>'.lyceum.com.br).
settings.sessionExpiredMessage: Your Lyceum session has expired. Reconnect to keep accessing grades and academic history.
settings.errorLoginFailed: Couldn't complete the Lyceum login. Try again.

lyceumAcademico.faltasNote: Shows whatever absence data the Lyceum grade report exposes. If the portal only returns a count/percentage (no specific days), that's all that shows up here.
lyceumAcademico.boletimCurrentOnlyNote: Lyceum only exposes the current semester's grades — the portal API has no period filter.
lyceumAcademico.sessionExpired: Your Lyceum session expired. Reconnect to keep seeing your academic data.
